column1: # Evidence Log: Track all research findings with citations
# - timestamp: When evidence was collected (ISO format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS)
# - source_type: journal | conference | book | web | preprint
# - key_finding: Main takeaway from this source (1-2 sentences)
# - confidence: high | medium | low (based on source quality and clarity)
# Format: BibTeX (@article{...}) or APA (Author (Year). Title.) recommended

column4: key_finding
column6: notes

column4: agents.md is prose-only Markdown: no schema, no official linter, no conformance suite, no version number. Only normative content: filename, root placement, nearest-file-wins for nested files.
column6: RQ-01; sources agentsmd-site, agentsmd-repo

column3: Linux Foundation (2025). Formation of the Agentic AI Foundation.
column4: agents.md governed by AAIF (Linux Foundation) since Dec 2025; created by OpenAI Aug 2025; 30+ adopting tools, ~60k repos claimed.
column6: RQ-01; adoption figure is OpenAI-reported, not independently verified; source lf-aaif

column3: OpenAI (2026). Custom instructions with AGENTS.md - Codex.
column4: Concatenation order, 32 KiB cap, override files etc. are per-vendor (Codex) behavior, not part of the standard.
column6: RQ-01; source codex-agentsmd

column3: OpenClaw (2026). Agent workspace + System prompt + Default AGENTS.md docs.
column4: OpenClaw AGENTS.md is a runtime workspace policy file: injected first into every session system prompt, 20KB/file 60KB total truncation, default template ships SOP-grade safety rules; sub-agents receive only AGENTS.md+TOOLS.md.
column6: RQ-01/RQ-04; sources oc-workspace, oc-sysprompt, oc-agents-default

column4: Cross-vendor AGENTS.md and OpenClaw AGENTS.md share only a filename; they diverge on discovery, trigger, precedence, consumer, and testable surface. Verdict: two adapters.
column6: RQ-01 DECISION; synthesis of agentsmd-site, codex-agentsmd, oc-workspace, oc-sysprompt

column4: Skills spec is now an open standard at agentskills.io (Apache-2.0/CC-BY-4.0); anthropics/skills spec file is a redirect pointer. Normative: name 1-64 chars [a-z0-9-] matching dir name; description 1-1024 chars; optional license/compatibility/metadata/allowed-tools (experimental); 3-level progressive disclosure.
column6: RQ-02; sources as-spec, anth-skills, as-repo

column4: The skills spec is UNVERSIONED: no releases, no changelog. skills-ref reference validator is the de-facto schema (no JSON Schema published).
column6: RQ-02 RISK; pin a commit SHA; unversioned inferred from absence; sources as-repo, as-skills-ref

column3: Agent Skills (2026). Client Showcase; OpenAI Codex skills docs.
column4: ~42 products adopted Agent Skills incl. OpenAI Codex, Gemini CLI, Cursor, GitHub Copilot, JetBrains, AWS Kiro.
column6: RQ-02; sources as-clients, openai-codex-skills

column4: Official trigger-testing methodology exists: 8-10 should-trigger + 8-10 near-miss should-not-trigger queries, N=3 runs, trigger-rate >=0.5 threshold, 60/40 train/val split, <=5 iterations. Guidance-level, not a conformance standard.
column6: RQ-02; muster can cite this as upstream methodology; source as-opt-desc

column3: OpenAI (2026-01-22). Testing Agent Skills Systematically with Evals.
column4: OpenAI ships an equivalent Codex skills eval method: explicit-invocation, implicit-invocation, negative-control tests over JSONL traces.
column6: RQ-02; source openai-eval-skills

column3: Huang et al. (2024). MetaTool (ICLR 2024); BFCL irrelevance detection.
column4: Academic analogs of trigger testing exist: tool-use-or-not decisions with similar-choice confusion sets (MetaTool) and must-not-call abstention metrics (BFCL).
column6: RQ-02; sources metatool, bfcl

column4: Static skill linters exist (skills-ref validate, agent-skill-linter ~20 rules) but no tool unifies static spec conformance + behavioral trigger conformance into one suite. Gap confirmed.
column6: RQ-02 GAP; sources as-skills-ref, skill-linter

column3: rokoss21 (2026). soul.md RFC-1 repo + CHANGELOG + GitHub API metadata.
column4: RFC-1 frozen at 1.0.0-rc1 (2026-02-11); repo dormant since creation day (all 3 commits same day, 0 issues); promised 1.0.0 deliverables (ref impl, CTS-1 fixtures) never shipped.
column6: RQ-03 RISK for v1 positioning; sources rfc1-repo, rfc1-changelog, rfc1-api

column3: ClawSouls (2026). SoulSpec v0.5 + repo API + soulspec.org.
column4: SoulSpec latest is v0.5 (2026-02-24); soulspec.org still advertises v0.4; ClawSouls org active (pushes 2026-06-11). Versions are cumulative diffs, not self-contained.
column6: RQ-03; sources soulspec-v05, soulspec-api, soulspec-org

column4: SoulSpec and RFC-1 are unrelated lineages: zero cross-citation either direction. SoulSpec requires soul.json manifest + multi-file package; has no frontmatter, no composition/merge rules, no published JSON Schema, minimal RFC-2119 language.
column6: RQ-03; sources soulspec-v05, soulspec-v03, rfc1-repo

column4: SpecAdapter boundary reusable as designed; rfc1 implementation not reusable for SoulSpec: parse new (JSON), validate new (muster-authored schema), resolve degenerates to package assembly, thresholds/triggers have no SoulSpec equivalent. Behavioral lanes stay RFC-1-only.
column6: RQ-03 DECISION: separate adapter, core reuse only; source muster-core

column4: Exact injection order exists only in source code: AGENTS(10) SOUL(20) IDENTITY(30) USER(40) TOOLS(50) BOOTSTRAP(60) MEMORY(70); HEARTBEAT dynamic after prompt-cache boundary. Hardcoded, not configurable (issue #65438). Docs file-listing order differs and is not normative.
column6: RQ-04; load-order checks can cite source code or muster rubric only; sources oc-source-order, oc-issue-order, oc-sysprompt

column3: OpenClaw (2026). Agent workspace docs (live + repo mirror).
column4: MEMORY.md privacy rule is official-docs verbatim: 'Only load MEMORY.md in the main, private session (not shared/group contexts).' Directly citable for the privacy-boundary probe.
column6: RQ-04; sources oc-workspace, oc-workspace-repo

column4: TOOLS.md is guidance-only ('does not control tool availability') - drift lint is a muster-rubric check, not an upstream-violation check. Truncation: bootstrapMaxChars=20000, bootstrapTotalMaxChars=60000 (third-party 150k figures wrong).
column6: RQ-04; source oc-workspace

column3: OpenClaw (2026). Heartbeat gateway docs + HEARTBEAT template.
column4: HEARTBEAT.md semantics documented: default prompt, interval 30m default (1h under Anthropic OAuth), HEARTBEAT_OK quiet-ack with ackMaxChars=300, empty file skips run, 0m disables and removes from bootstrap.
column6: RQ-04; interval varies by auth mode - checks must read config; sources oc-heartbeat, oc-heartbeat-tpl

column4: All OpenClaw workspace files have official docs with stable URLs (class a); load order is source-only (class b); conflict precedence documented NOWHERE - the 'undefined precedence' static finding is validated. Project renamed twice Jan 2026; pin citations to commit SHAs.
column6: RQ-04 DECISION; sources oc-workspace, oc-sysprompt, oc-source-order, oc-wikipedia

column3: A2A Project (2026). A2A Protocol Specification v1.0.
column4: A2A spec v1.0.0 (2026-03-12, LF governance); protobuf a2a.proto is the single normative definition, published JSON schema explicitly non-normative; well-known URI is /.well-known/agent-card.json (agent.json obsolete). Spec mandates card accuracy: interface declarations MUST be accurate, undeclared capabilities MUST yield specific errors, auth per declared schemes.
column6: RQ-05; sources a2a-spec, a2a-releases

column4: An OFFICIAL A2A conformance suite already exists: a2a-tck (pytest, v1.0.0, active) does card schema validation, discovery, capability-conditional behavioral tests across three transports, RFC-2119-leveled reporting. Corrects the brief's assumption that no prior art exists.
column6: RQ-05 DECISION: deprioritize generic card validation; residual gap = signed-card live verification, skill-level probing, auth negatives, CI monitoring; source a2a-tck

column4: Field evidence of widespread card/behavior mismatch in deployed agents (issue #1755); third-party validators exist but lag spec (capiscio at v0.3.0).
column6: RQ-05; contract-testing value confirmed but TCK owns the center; sources a2a-issue, capiscio

column4: No major eval harness (promptfoo, DeepEval, OpenAI Evals, Inspect, LangSmith, Braintrust, Ragas, Giskard, lm-eval, HELM, Anthropic Console) validates agent definition FILES against specs - all test runtime outputs of an opaque target.
column6: RQ-06; sources pf-asserts, de-metrics, oai-graders, inspect-scorers

column4: Counterevidence found: a static-linter ecosystem does file-spec conformance (cclint, agent-skill-linter, Agnix, AgentShield); AgentLinter even flags cross-file persona mismatch (SOUL.md vs CLAUDE.md) and permission conflicts - static and heuristic only.
column6: RQ-06; AgentLinter is the closest competitor, targets OpenClaw-ecosystem files; hands-on audit pending; sources agentlinter, cclint, skill-linter

column4: Brief's gap claim is overbroad. Defensible narrowed claim: no eval HARNESS combines file-spec conformance + cross-layer composition analysis + behavioral verification of the composed stack with CI semantics. Memory and schedule layers covered by nothing at all.
column6: RQ-06 DECISION: rewrite positioning claim; synthesis of all RQ-06 sources

column4: Assertion-vocabulary reuse targets: promptfoo richest (trajectory/tool-sequence asserts, --repeat without k-of-n reducer); Inspect has native k-of-n via epochs + at_least reducers; DeepEval agentic metrics are judge-based.
column6: RQ-06; Inspect reducer detail snippet-verified only; sources pf-asserts, inspect-scorers, de-metrics

column3: Wallace et al. (2024). The Instruction Hierarchy. arXiv:2404.13208.
column4: Instruction-precedence conflict handling is an established research line: OpenAI trains system>developer>user>tool privilege levels on synthetic conflicting-instruction data.
column6: RQ-07; source instr-hierarchy

column4: Best models score <50% on multi-tier privilege-conflict resolution (ManyIH-Bench, 46 real agents); SysBench documents system-message adherence degradation and multi-turn instability.
column6: RQ-07; ManyIH verified via snippets only; sources manyih, sysbench

column4: Within-context conflict detection has direct precedent: WIRE runs SAT-style conflict witnesses over rules extracted from one composed policy (35.4% joint compliance); Arbiter detects block-pair interference in real agent system prompts (4 direct contradictions found in Claude Code's own prompt; $0.27/run).
column6: RQ-07; closest prior art to muster's static cross-layer lint; both fetched directly; sources wire, arbiter

column3: Deshpande et al. (2023). Toxicity in ChatGPT. EMNLP Findings.
column4: Persona assignment raises toxicity up to 6x - the canonical persona-erosion citation.
column6: RQ-07; rule-survival test justification; source deshpande2023

column4: Persona prompts cut refusal rates 50-70%; adversarial history reshapes induced persona; persona drift within ~8 turns (attention decay); sycophancy is a trained behavior favoring user-pleasing over policy.
column6: RQ-07; numbers match abstracts, not all re-verified against full PDFs; sources persona-jailbreak, phish, persona-drift, sycophancy

column4: No mature CI-grade prompt-contradiction linter exists - only research prototypes (Arbiter, WIRE) and hobby linters. Open gap for muster's static cross-layer lint.
column6: RQ-07 DECISION; sources arbiter, agentlinter

column3: Zheng et al. (2023). MT-Bench judge study; Wang et al. (2023). Fair Evaluators; Judging the Judges (2026).
column4: LLM judges reach ~80% human agreement but exhibit position, verbosity (+17.3% length bias), self-enhancement and ~12 catalogued biases; order-swap and rubric anchoring are the documented mitigations.
column6: RQ-08; encode in muster judge graders; sources mtbench, fair-eval, judging-judges

column3: Souly et al. (2024). StrongREJECT. NeurIPS 2024 D&B.
column4: Naive string-match refusal detection badly overstates jailbreak success (empty jailbreaks) - refusal QUALITY grading needs a calibrated rubric evaluator, not regex alone.
column6: RQ-08; refusal checks are hybrid binary+judge; source strongreject

column4: SOP rules can compile to executable oracle verifiers over tool-call traces (SOPBench: 167 tools, 900+ cases, rule-based graders); tau-bench's pass^k (conjunctive over k trials) is the citable standard for stochastic compliance - pass^8 <25% even when pass^1 ~50%.
column6: RQ-08 DECISION: binary/judge line at trace decidability; adopt pass^k for safety-critical rules; sources sopbench, taubench

column4: Objectively gradable rule classes: never-call-tool-X, tool-order, argument constraints, confirm-before-destructive (event order), output format, exact leakage strings. Judge-required: refusal quality, paraphrased leakage, tone/persona adherence. No citable formal taxonomy exists - muster must publish its own rubric.
column6: RQ-08; taxonomy is muster's own synthesis grounded in practice, not a single citable framework; sources sopbench, taubench, pf-deterministic, inspect-scorers

column3: License verification sweep (GitHub API spdx, HF tags, raw LICENSE files).
column4: Clean-to-vendor corpora (verified 2026-06-12): InjecAgent (MIT, 1054 agent tool-injection cases), AgentDojo (MIT, 629 security cases), gandalf_ignore_instructions (MIT, 1000), deepset/prompt-injections (Apache-2.0, 662), LLMail-Inject (MIT, ~208k raw), JBB-Behaviors (MIT, 100+100).
column6: RQ-09 DECISION shortlist; re-verify at vendoring time; sources injecagent, agentdojo, gandalf, deepset-pi, llmail, jbb

column3: PINT; promptfoo red-team; BIPIA; garak license notes.
column4: Not vendorable: PINT dataset (deliberately private), promptfoo red-team payloads (runtime-generated, partly remote API), BIPIA WebQA/Summarization contexts (upstream restrictions; attack templates OK). garak Apache-2.0 but aggregates third-party probe data - per-file provenance check required.
column6: RQ-09; sources pint, pf-redteam, bipia, garak

column4: Only AgentDojo, InjecAgent, LLMail-Inject (and BIPIA partially) cover AGENT scenarios (tool use, scope escape, exfiltration); deepset/gandalf/JBB/HarmBench/AdvBench are chat-level direct injection or jailbreak.
column6: RQ-09; sources agentdojo, injecagent, llmail, bipia

column4: Sequencing decision revises the brief's hypothesis: Skills first (real open spec, neutral governance, ~42 clients, official trigger methodology, no unified conformance suite - the v1 RFC-1 playbook with a larger audience), OpenClaw SOP second (biggest safety payoff; persona+SOP unlocks rule-survival headline), cross-layer third. A2A waits (official TCK owns the center).
column6: RQ-10 DECISION; SOP-first remains defensible if cross-layer headline is the near-term priority
